assertion_results.text: Data extraction correctly identifies CVE-2026-31812, library quinn-proto, affected range < 0.11.14, and fixed version 0.11.14 from the Vulnerability issue (Step 1)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md contains: CVE ID = CVE-2026-31812, Vulnerable library = quinn-proto, Affected version range = < 0.11.14, Fixed version = 0.11.14 (lines 7, 11-13)

assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as Cargo based on the library name and component context (Step 1 Ecosystem detection)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md section 'Ecosystem Detection' states: 'The vulnerable library is quinn-proto, a Rust crate. This maps to the Cargo ecosystem from the Ecosystem Mappings table in the 2.2.x stream's security-matrix.md.' Lock File is identified as Cargo.lock (lines 29-36)

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the security-matrix.md supportability matrix — no versions are skipped (Important Rule 4)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md includes all 7 versions across both streams: 2.1.0, 2.1.1 (stream 2.1.x) and 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4 (stream 2.2.x). Both streams are loaded from their respective security-matrix.md files (lines 5-8, 14-21)

assertion_results.text: Version impact analysis uses pinned commit tags from the supportability matrix for each version, not branch HEAD (Important Rule 13)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md Tag column shows specific pinned tags for each version: v0.3.8 (2.1.0), v0.3.12 (2.1.1), v0.4.5 (2.2.0), v0.4.8 (2.2.1), v0.4.9 (2.2.2), v0.4.11 (2.2.3), v0.4.12 (2.2.4). Lock file checks reference these tags, not branch HEAD (lines 16-21)

assertion_results.text: Version impact table identifies retag versions and carries forward the affected status from the original version (Important Rule 5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md row for 2.2.2 states: 'retag of 2.2.1 (same as v0.4.8: 0.11.12)' with Affected = YES, correctly carrying forward the affected status from 2.2.1 which ships quinn-proto 0.11.12 (line 20)

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction proposes replacing PSIRT-assigned versions with lock-file-verified versions, showing the diff (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md shows the diff: 'Current: [RHTPA 2.0.0]' vs 'Proposed: [RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2]' (lines 23-26). Includes detailed rationale with per-version lock file analysis at pinned commits (lines 28-34)

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction references version names from the supportability matrix rather than hardcoded Jira version IDs (Important Rule 6)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md uses version names 'RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2' throughout. The PROPOSAL Jira Mutation section explicitly uses placeholder IDs: '"id": "<jira-id-for-RHTPA-2.2.0>"' etc., with the note 'Jira version IDs would be discovered dynamically via getJiraIssueTypeMetaWithFields (Step 3.1). These are placeholder references -- the actual IDs are resolved at execution time.' (lines 39-50)

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks include Repository, Target Branch, acceptance criteria, and dependency linkage between upstream and downstream tasks (Important Rule 9)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 1 includes: Repository = rhtpa-backend (line 35), Target Branch = release/0.4.z (line 39), Acceptance Criteria checklist (lines 63-65), and dependency link to TC-8001 (lines 85-89). Task 2 includes: Repository = rhtpa-release.0.4.z (line 113), Target Branch = main (line 117), Acceptance Criteria (lines 137-138), and Blocks dependency link from upstream to downstream (lines 166-172)

assertion_results.text: For Cargo ecosystem, remediation creates two tasks: upstream backport task and downstream propagation subtask with Blocks dependency (Important Rule 8)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md creates Task 1 (upstream backport: 'Remediate CVE-2026-31812: bump quinn-proto to 0.11.14') and Task 2 (downstream propagation: 'Propagate CVE-2026-31812 fix: update rhtpa-backend ref in rhtpa-release.0.4.z'). Blocks dependency is explicitly created: 'jira.create_link(inwardIssue: <upstream-task-key>, outwardIssue: <downstream-task-key>, type: "Blocks")' (lines 166-172)

assertion_results.text: The analysis presents triage recommendations as proposed actions — framing Affects Versions changes, label additions, and status transitions as proposals rather than executed mutations (Guardrails)
assertion_results.evidence: All mutations are consistently prefixed with 'PROPOSAL:' throughout the outputs. affects-versions.md: 'PROPOSAL: Jira Mutation' (line 39), 'PROPOSAL: Correction Comment' (line 52). remediation.md: 'PROPOSAL: Create Jira Task' (lines 17, 98, 193, 277), 'PROPOSAL: Link to Vulnerability issue' (lines 83, 156), 'PROPOSAL: Link downstream blocked by upstream' (lines 165, 349), 'PROPOSAL: Add ai-cve-triaged label' (line 363), 'PROPOSAL: Post summary comment' (line 372), 'PROPOSAL: Post description digest comment' (lines 76, 149, 255, 333). No mutations are presented as already executed.
